title: Coleus subspicatus
intro: Coleus subspicatus, also known as Coleus aromaticus or Coleus scutellarioides, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly referred to as a type of aromatic coleus. Traditionally, Coleus subspicatus has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Coleus subspicatus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coleus subspicatus is often made into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Coleus subspicatus is classified as a species within the genus Coleus, which belongs to the family Lamiaceae. This family is commonly known as the mint or deadnettle family. Coleus subspicatus is a member of the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants that include mint, basil, and sage. The classification of Coleus subspicatus is further specified as Coleus subspicatus (Vahl) Backer, indicating that it is a species that has undergone reclassification. The classification reflects the plant's botanical relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Coleus subspicatus, also known as Coleus forskohlii, has been found to have various medicinal uses. The plant contains a compound called forskolin, which has been shown to have potential in reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. Forskolin may also have anti-inflammatory properties and has been studied for its potential in treating conditions such as asthma and allergies. Additionally, forskolin has been used to treat glaucoma and to stimulate the production of a hormone that helps to dissolve fat cells. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Coleus subspicatus for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Coleus subspicatus is known for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving sleep quality. Additionally, Coleus subspicatus has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as fever and digestive issues. The plant contains a compound called forskolin, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. constituents: Coleus subspicatus contains active constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's leaves are rich in vitamins A and E, which contribute to its potential health benefits. parts: Coleus subspicatus, also known as Coleus forskohlii, is a plant species with medicinal properties.
